title: 2 Recession-Resistant Dividend Stocks to Buy Now While They're Still Cheap
author: Micah Zimmerman
description: The article recommends two recession-resistant dividend stocks: Waste Connections (WCN), a solid waste company with durable pricing power and contract structures, and Brookfield Infrastructure Partners (BIP), which owns inflation-indexed and regulated assets globally with growing data infrastructure exposure. Both offer reliable dividend growth at reasonable valuations without premium pricing.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Recommended as recession-proof with essential non-discretionary services, durable pricing power through CPI-linked escalators, consistent double-digit dividend growth, and strong contract structures. Trading at reasonable valuations with solid operational fundamentals.

Recommended for recession resilience through 90% inflation-indexed or regulated cash flows, recent 6% distribution increase, global diversification across utilities and infrastructure, and strategic exposure to AI-driven data infrastructure growth. Attractive entry point with long-term growth potential.

title: Investment Manager Sheds $45.7 Million Worth of Software Stock, According to Latest SEC Filing
author: Jake Lerch
description: Conestoga Capital Advisors sold 634,534 shares of SPS Commerce (SPSC) worth approximately $45.7 million in Q1 2026, reducing its stake to near-negligible levels. The stock has declined 59.8% over the past year but trades at a P/S ratio of 2.8x—the lowest in over a decade—while the company maintains solid revenue growth and invests in AI capabilities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed signals: negative price performance (down 59.8% YoY) and major institutional stake reduction suggest weakness, but the company shows resilient revenue growth ($492M to $750M), attractive valuation metrics (2.8x P/S near decade lows), and strategic AI investments. The sale appears tactical rather than a vote of no confidence.
Mentioned as Conestoga Capital's top holding ($233.17M, 4.7% of AUM) with no specific news or performance data provided in the article.
Listed as second-largest holding in Conestoga Capital's portfolio ($222.51M, 4.4% of AUM) with no specific news or performance data provided.
